title: AI Expense Trackers for Solopreneurs in 2026: Keeper vs Expensify vs Dext vs QuickBooks Self-Employed
description: Receipt scanning, mileage tracking, and tax-ready reports—four expense tools compared on pricing, automation accuracy, and who they're built for.
tldr: Dext leads on receipt-capture accuracy (99.9%) and accounting integrations but lacks public pricing. FreshBooks delivers the most complete expense-plus-invoicing stack at $12.90/mo (Plus plan, 70% off for 4 months). Expensify and QuickBooks Self-Employed both failed to publish pricing in their vendor pages. Keeper was unreachable.

body.text: Solopreneurs need expense tracking that doesn't require a Saturday morning spreadsheet session. Receipt scanning, mileage logs, and tax-category assignment should happen automatically. We compared five platforms—Keeper, Expensify, Dext, QuickBooks Self-Employed, and FreshBooks—on published pricing, AI accuracy claims, and integration depth. Two vendors provided no pricing. One claims 99.9% extraction accuracy. One bundles expense tracking inside a full invoicing suite.

We reviewed vendor pricing pages, feature lists, and published accuracy claims. No fabricated test counts. If pricing wasn't listed, we note that. If a vendor cited specific accuracy rates or integration numbers, we cite them verbatim. Integration claims come from vendor-published lists. User review excerpts come from testimonials on vendor pages or linked review platforms (G2, Capterra, Trustpilot).
Vendor page was unreachable. No pricing, feature list, or integration claims could be verified. Omitted from detailed comparison.

body.name: Expensify
body.price: Pricing not published on reviewed page; contact vendor
pros: Integrates with QuickBooks Online, NetSuite, Xero, Sage Intacct, QuickBooks Desktop, FinancialForce (per integration section)
Expensify Visa Commercial Card available (issued by The Bancorp Bank or Transact Payments for EEA/UK residents)
Money transmission services via Expensify Payments LLC (NMLS ID: 2017010)
cons: Pricing page displayed no plan tiers or monthly costs
No feature descriptions or receipt-capture accuracy claims found in reviewed content
Vendor page consisted primarily of integration dropdowns and footer legal text

body.name: Dext
body.price: Pricing not published on main page; trial available, contact vendor for plan costs
pros: Claims 99.9% accuracy on receipt and invoice data extraction via AI
Over 30 accounting software integrations listed, connects with 11,500+ banks and institutions
Mobile app for receipt submission, mileage tracking, and team expense approval
Vault document storage and email submission of receipts/invoices
Rated 4.8 on G2 (1,000+ reviews), 4.8 on Capterra (1,100+ ratings), 4.8 on Trustpilot (6,280+ reviews)
2024 Xero small business app of the year (US & UK)
cons: No plan pricing or per-month costs published on main page
Feature list emphasizes automation but no comparisons to competitor extraction accuracy
Best suited for users who already use supported accounting platforms (integration-dependent workflow)

body.text: Vendor page was unreachable. No pricing, feature list, or integration claims could be verified. Omitted from detailed comparison.

body.name: FreshBooks
body.price: Lite $6.90/mo (70% off for 4 months, was $23), Plus $12.90/mo (70% off for 4 months, was $43), Premium $21.00/mo (70% off for 4 months, was $70), Select (contact for pricing)
pros: Expense tracking bundled with invoicing, time-tracking, proposals, and retainers
Mobile mileage tracking, automated bank import, and expense receipt scanning on all paid plans
Plus plan ($12.90/mo during promo) includes accountant access, receipt scanning, financial reports, and 50-client limit
Premium ($21.00/mo during promo) adds unlimited clients, bill receipt scanning with line-item capture, project profitability tracking
Accept payments via credit/debit cards, ACH bank transfer, Apple Pay, Google Pay, Buy Now Pay Later (Affirm, Afterpay)
Select plan includes 2 free team member accounts and dedicated phone support
cons: No published accuracy rate for receipt data extraction
Advanced Payments add-on costs $20/mo (Lite/Plus/Premium), included only on Select
Team members cost $11/mo per user on all plans except Select
Lite plan caps at 5 billable clients; insufficient for growing freelancers

items: If you need the highest published receipt-extraction accuracy and deep accounting integrations: Dext (99.9% claimed accuracy, 30+ integrations, but no public pricing—request a quote).
If you want expense tracking plus full invoicing, time-tracking, and proposals in one platform: FreshBooks Plus at $12.90/mo (50 clients, receipt scanning, accountant access, financial reports).
If you need unlimited clients and line-item bill capture: FreshBooks Premium at $21.00/mo (adds project profitability and customizable email templates).
If you need only expense tracking and your accountant already uses a supported platform: Dext (integration-first design, but expect higher cost for business-grade features).
If you're a single-person operation with fewer than 5 clients and minimal invoicing: FreshBooks Lite at $6.90/mo (basic invoicing, expense tracking, tax reports).
Expensify: Pricing page displayed no plan costs or feature descriptions—impossible to evaluate value without a sales call.
Keeper and QuickBooks Self-Employed: Vendor pages unreachable; cannot substantiate any feature or pricing claims.
FreshBooks Lite for growing freelancers: 5-client cap becomes a blocker faster than expected; Plus ($12.90/mo) is a safer starting point.
